content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 15, 1991

E. Jane Pool
City of Encinatas
City Clerk and Clerk of the Sanitary Districts
527 Encinatas Boulevard
Encinatas, CA 92024

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-346

Dear Ms. Pool:

You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act").1/

Your request is for clarification concerning the proposed
amendment to Regulation 18730 which if amended provides that an
individual does not need to be designated in a conflict of
interest code for an agency provided: (1) he or she is already
designated in a code for another agency; (2) the geographical
jurisdiction of the agencies is the same; (3) the disclosure
assigned in the code is the same as is required by Section 87200;
and (4) the filing officer would be the same for both agencies.

QUESTION

Will the proposed amendment to Regulation 18730 preclude the
need to designate individuals in the conflict of interest codes
for the sanitary, water and fire protection districts? If so,
should their designation be removed at the annual review of the
codes for these boards?

CONCLUSION

The sanitary, water and fire protection districts do not meet
the criteria as set out in the proposed amendments to Regulation
18730. As such, the city council members who serve as the board
members for the districts, as well as any other designated

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-91-346
Page 2

positions, must remain designated in each district's conflict of
interest code.

FACTS

The City of Encinatas was incorporated in October, 1986. At
that time, the city acquired two previously existing sanitary
districts, a fire protection district and a water district. In
each instance, the boundaries of the districts were not
coterminous with the new city, each lying partly within and partly
outside the city limits. The filing officers for the water
district and the fire protection district are not the same.

The members of the city council serve as the Boards of
Directors for all four of the districts and are currently
designated in each of the district's conflict of interest codes.

It is your belief that the two sanitary districts meet all
the criteria as set out in the proposed Regulation 18730(b)
Section 3, except for the discrepancy in geographical
jurisdiction.

ANALYSIS

In determining whether the board members for the districts,
who are members of the city council, qualify under the proposed
amendments to Regulation 18730, specific criteria must be met.

At its August 6, 1991, meeting, the Commission amended
Regulation 18730 so that it provides that designated employees who
are designated in a conflict of interest code for more than one
agency, need not be subject to disclosure obligations under both
codes if all of the following apply:

A) The geographical jurisdiction of one agency is the same
as or is wholly included within the jurisdiction of the other
agency;

B) The disclosure assigned in the code is the same as that
required under Article 2 of Chapter 7 of the Political Reform Act,
Government Code Sections 87200; and

C) The filing officer is the same for both agencies.

It is stated in your letter that the district's geographical
jurisdictions are not wholly contained within the boundaries of
the city of Encinatas. The jurisdiction for the city of Encinatas
is the city. The jurisdictions of the various districts encompass
areas beyond the city's boundaries. As such, the districts do not
meet the criteria in the regulation and designated board members
must continue to file disclosure statements for the district.

A city councilmember who sits on the various boards may
expand his or her statement of economic interest to cover

Our File No. A-91-346
Page 3


reportable interests in both jurisdictions and file copies of this
expanded statement, with an original signature, in both
jurisdictions. (Regulation 18730.)

     I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Scott Hallabrin
                                   Acting General Counsel

                                   [illegible]
                                   By: Cheryl A. Hoff
                                       Staff Services Analyst
                                       Legal Division

Enclosures
